entry.title: Re: SLT Servers High space / memory utilization
entry.content:
1. Make sure no old data in ODQMON and all obsolete subscriptions have been deleted. See note 2502498 - Huge amount of data in ODQDATA table
  2. Follow note 2300483 - ODQ - performance of ODQDATA_V and ODQTSNLOG to create indexes:
    • Secondary index on the table ODQDATA: Fields: CLIENT, MODELNAME, QUEUENAME
    • Secondary index on the table ODQTSNLOG: CLIENT, TID

  3. Apply note 3213659 - ODQ_CLEANUP: Runtime error SYSTEM_NO_ROLL in delete_queue_data

  4. Execute ODQ_CLEANUP job with different values of Retention period to clear the data one period by one period
    [For example]: Suppose that there're old data over than 20 months:
    • Delete them "month-wise" starting with a retention time of 19 months (24h * 30 days/month * 19 months = 13,680 hours to start with):
      Recovery  13,680 Hours  <-----
      Data with low relevance 10 Days
      Data with average relevance 31 Days

      If this is successful it should have deleted confirmed data older than 19 months.

    • Change the variant always reducing the retention time by 720 hours (a month with 30 days):
      Recovery  12,960 Hours  <----- 13,680 - 720
      Data with low relevance 10 Days
      Data with average relevance 31 Days

    • Next retention time should be reduced step by step from 12,240, 11,520, 10,800, ... , to 720 for the last month finally.

entry.title: Re: How to select a specific session to do SAP GUI Scripting activities inside it
entry.content:
Hello @ndionisio_giv,

thank you very much for your comment and your kind words.

First of all congratulations that you decided to getting involved with programming. This is a good way to effectively realize your own automations.

You have detect a very interesting behavior that I have never noticed before. A session that is being debugged blocks the call of connection.Children(CLng(j)) - in your case in line 55 - to get the session. I tried it with dotNET and here it is the same behavior. Scripting Tracker works on a completely different basis that cannot be compared with these approaches. I will think about how this problem can possibly be solved, but I don't want to promise a solution.

See you later, best regards
Stefan

entry.title: SAP Joule
entry.content: Do we have any trail version of Joule. Can we do a POC

entry.title: Re: Coupons not showing in SAP Customer Checkout
entry.content:
Solved.

I synchronized coupons using "Discount" under "Synchronization" in tab in Customer checkout POS

entry.title: Re: In the S4hana Public cloud,the enterprise project con...
entry.content:
Hi Devi ,

 

Can you please refer to the below KBA's for the mentioned issue.

3329470 - Unable to post time for a service performer

3052414 - Task is not available in the Manage My Timesheet app

 

Thanks,

Monisha.

entry.title: Re: Refresh of CL_SALV_TREE
entry.content: Why is it so difficult? Simply call the display( ) method again after changing the tree data. And you will be happy 🙂

entry.title: Re: Refresh of CL_SALV_TREE
entry.content:
TRY.
      lt_nodes = go_alv_tree->get_nodes( )->get_all_nodes( ).
      LOOP AT lt_nodes INTO DATA(ls_node).
        lcl_item = ls_node-node->get_hierarchy_item( ).
        IF lcl_item->is_checked( ) = abap_true.
          ls_node-node->delete( ).
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
      go_alv_tree->display( ).   "Refresh tree on screen, after delete nodes
    CATCH cx_salv_msg.
      EXIT.
  ENDTRY.

A simple example of updating a tree after removing some nodes.

entry.title: Re: A very strange SQL requirement
entry.content: ABAP SQL probably not, but I think that it's possible for native SQL (HANA or other database), I already saw same kind of SQL questions in Stack Overflow, also I'm sure it's possible with HANA SQLScript (it's a programming language so anything is possible).

entry.title: Re: SAP API Management: Discover Integration Flows from CPI tenants and auto-generate APIs
entry.content:
Dear Team,

Scenario: external system ( API consumer) ==> CPI ( API provider) ==> SuccessFactors

We generated the API endpoint in CPI and shared with external system along clientid and secrect . But, due to special chars in cleintid & secrect, they are not able to use them. Hence, they are asking CPI team to generate without special chars. As i understood , it is not possible. 

Hence, i am thinking different approach like to generate apikey in API management and connect to CPI endpoint (iflow) . I believe it is possible. Please share relavant blog API management connection to CPI iflow?

Please ping your skills if anyone aware.

 

Vasu

entry.title: Re: Bank Reconciliation using SAP standard tempate in FIO...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

I didn't get exactly what you do. Do you have a posting rule or processing rule in place to handle your posting automatically?

I would propose to create an automatic processing rule: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/d6fe6526b26f46e59d9d1dd0f7427fe3.html?version=2402.500

 

 

Can you share some screenshots explaining what exactly you mean?

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Financials Development

entry.title: Re: Secure Communication between EWM-MFS & PLC
entry.content:
Thanks much DominikTylczyn for your feedback. There was a typo. Correct SAP NOTE is 3132199. 

Without TLS, telegram message is not secure & I do not believe PCo has the ability to decrypt encrypted messages. So does not seem to solve the purpose here which Web dispatcher does. Happy to hear if i am missing anything here. 

Thanks 

Anubhab

entry.title: Re: WWI servers in RISE
entry.content:
Hello

1.) please check OSS Marketplace and search for the OSS Note describing  "how to do a set up of a WWI server"; by checking this OSS note you will get more insight about the WWI set up

2.) WWI as such is a "Windows" based solution. So as long as on the server you install a "windows" OS anything is fine (according to my knowledge).

C.B.

entry.title: how to add attribute TAXNUMBER to component BP_HEAD_SEARCH as new search field
entry.content:
I have a requirement to add TAXNUMBER as a new search field in the BP_HEAD_SEARCH component (in the BP_HEAD_SEARCH/SearchHelp view). So far I have already seen that TAXNUMBER is available in the related result entity BuilHeader (via the relation BuilTaxNumberRel).
The question now is whether someone can explain to me the steps on how to add the TAXNUMBER field to the SEARCH context node in the right way. As I am not an expert and relatively new to working with the component workbench in CRM (and in S/4), some screen shots would be very helpful.

could any experts help me? thanks in advance.

Regards,
Chris

E.g. if you do research you will find threads as: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/generating-labels-with-wwi/qaq-p/6967370

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/generating-labels-with-wwi/qaq-p/6967370

and many others.

With best wshes

C.B.

entry.title: Re: Access Policies: Restrict Access to Integration Artifacts in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Chandra,

please take a look wether your user has the role AccessAllAccessPoliciesArtifacts asigned (It's in the rolecollection PI_Administrator) If this is the case, restriction to Artifacts is not working.

Read this SAP note: https://me.sap.com/notes/3440861/E

Best regards

Christian

entry.title: Re: Analytical List Page Value help without ForeignKey Association
entry.content:
Junwu,

It is getting recognized in CDS. It is even visible in Active Annotations. But when exposed via /iwfnd/maint_services tcode the entity is not available in Metadata.

Additional Info: the system is S4 Hana 2022 fps02

The same Annotation is working if the service is exposed using service Binding and Service Definition.

entry.title: Re: Statistics of a blog post
entry.content:
You can see your own stats, or others, in scattered locations but not yet all your content in one page. First, go to the topic area. E.g. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap...

Breadcrumb trail to topic

 

Then, scroll down until your post is in view.

 

Stats at lower center of shot.

 

Page counts, comments, and kudos are shown per post.

entry.title: Re: First Half 2024 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central?

Have you tried employment differentiator to fix that ? It allows you to add up to 3 fields from jobinfo.

if position text is sync with a jobknfo custom field , it should do the trick.

entry.title: Understanding date condition in bods job
entry.content:
Hi All

I have seen a date condition in job transformation as below,

does it mean this condition would fetch data records 21 days prior to current data and 21 days later to current sate ?

Date field >= to_char(sysdate()-21, 'yyyymmdd')

and

Date field <= to_char(sysdate()+21, 'yyyymmdd')

 

Regards

Siba

I would like to know if there is ability to create automatic P.R with reference to reservations

the business scenario is the customer needs to create a reservation to issue goods on it the customer needs if there is no enough QTYs in the warehouse the reservation converted automatically to P.R

entry.title: Como Foi o Primeiro SAP Meet Up Recife
entry.content:
Text in English

Olá a todos! É com muita alegria que compartilho aqui no blog como foi o nosso primeiro evento SAP Meet up da comunidade SAP em Recife. Há muitos motivos para celebrar, desde a criação do nosso grupo local até o retorno dos eventos presenciais desde 2020. Além disso, tivemos discussões sobre grandes possibilidades futuras e, claro, foi ótimo rever colegas e amigos que participam da comunidade SAP há tanto tempo.

Ter uma comunidade forte na sua cidade é de extrema importância, pois facilita a organização de eventos e encontros. Muitas mentes pensam melhor juntas, e esse é o espírito do nosso grupo: adotar grandes ideias que todos podem oferecer para valorizar ainda mais a nossa comunidade SAP local. Isso se refletiu na prática, pois o evento foi organizado de maneira muito rápida, com apenas uma semana de preparação, mas superou nossas expectativas e se tornou um encontro muito agradável. Todos trouxeram muitas ideias e histórias que nos dão direção para futuras iniciativas na nossa comunidade.

Gostaria de agradecer especialmente ao Antônio Figueiredo, que foi fundamental na organização do evento e no convite aos participantes. Também foi muito bom contar com o apoio do Hélder Lins, presidente da GGTI, uma liderança de gestão de TI aqui em Recife, e de todos os outros amigos que marcaram presença.

Foi maravilhoso interagir com muitos profissionais SAP que me conheciam apenas do mundo virtual. Graças a esse encontro, pudemos estreitar laços na vida real. Aproveitei a oportunidade para fazer uma breve apresentação sobre as novidades da comunidade SAP e como ela oferece espaço para grupos locais compartilharem seus conhecimentos e agendarem eventos. Também falei sobre os três eventos anteriores da comunidade SAP em Recife. Muitos dos presentes já tinham participado desses eventos e estavam sentindo falta de mais iniciativas como essa.

Certamente, este ano teremos o retorno do nosso Inside Track aqui em Recife, onde receberemos vários palestrantes dispostos a compartilhar seus conhecimentos com a comunidade local. A interação durante o evento foi excelente, com muitas conversas bem-humoradas que tornaram a nossa noite muito relaxante na companhia de bons colegas. Além disso, discutimos soluções para projetos, como problemas em BTP, configuração de impostos e desafios gerais de projetos.

 

Foi muito bom ver essa diversidade de tópicos e assuntos na mesma mesa. Sem dúvida, o SAP Meet Up Recife veio para ficar. Já temos mais lugares disponíveis para nos reunirmos, com muitas pessoas dispostas a oferecer espaços maiores para esses encontros. A mensagem final é que o mercado profissional SAP precisa muito de mais informação, capacitação e aprendizado das boas práticas de implementação do sistema, além de desenvolver novas carreiras para estudantes e profissionais formados.

Agradeço a todos que puderam participar e aqueles que não puderam, não percam os próximos eventos. Sigam o nosso grupo para ficarem por dentro de todas as novidades. Abraço a todos!

entry.title: Re: Como Foi o Primeiro SAP Meet Up Recife
entry.content:
Recap of the First SAP Meet Up Recife

Hello everyone! I'm excited to share with you all how our first SAP community meet-up in Recife went. There are plenty of reasons to celebrate, from the creation of our local group to the return of in-person events since 2020. We also had fruitful discussions about future possibilities and, of course, it was fantastic to reconnect with colleagues and friends who have been active in the SAP community for a long time.

Having a strong community in your city is crucial because it makes organizing events and meet-ups much easier. Many minds working together generate better ideas, and that's the spirit of our group: to adopt and embrace great ideas that everyone can contribute to, enhancing our local SAP community. This was evident in practice as we organized the event very quickly, in just one week, and it exceeded our expectations, becoming a very enjoyable gathering filled with ideas and stories that will guide our future initiatives.

I'd like to extend a special thanks to Antônio Figueiredo, who was instrumental in organizing the event and inviting participants. It was also wonderful to have the support of Hélder, the president of GGTI, a leading IT management group in Recife, and all other friends who attended the event.

It was fantastic to interact with many SAP professionals who previously knew me only through the virtual world. Thanks to this meet-up, we were able to strengthen our connections in real life. I took the opportunity to give a brief presentation about the latest updates from the SAP community and how it provides space for local groups to share knowledge and schedule events. I also discussed the three previous SAP community events in Recife. Many attendees had participated in those events and were eager for more initiatives like this one.

We are definitely planning to bring back our Inside Track event to Recife this year, where we will welcome several speakers eager to share their valuable insights with the local community. The interaction during the event was excellent, with many humorous conversations making for a relaxing evening among good colleagues. Additionally, we had numerous discussions about project solutions, such as issues in BTP, tax configuration, and general project challenges.

It was wonderful to see such a diverse range of topics and discussions happening at the same table. The SAP Meet Up Recife is here to stay. We already have more venues lined up, with many people willing to provide larger spaces for these gatherings. The key takeaway is that the SAP professional market needs more information, training, and learning about best implementation practices, as well as the development of new careers for students and professionals. Developing this within our local community is essential, as it is from here that successful clients and partners will emerge.

Thank you to everyone who participated, and for those who couldn't make it, don't miss the next events. Follow our group to stay updated on all the latest news. Best regards to all!

entry.title: is there field for equipment number in P.R and P.O screen in MM
entry.content:
is there field for equipment number in P.R and P.O screen in MM 

My customer needs to have a reports for all purchased spare parts and services for an equipment

entry.title: Re: How to select a specific session to do SAP GUI Scripting activities inside it
entry.content:
Hello @ndionisio_giv,

here a solution to handle the behavior. This solution is in PowerShell, because the using of Windows Script Host (WSH) is disabled at my system. May I also recommend you to use PowerShell instead of VBScript of WSH. WSH is no longer further developed and PowerShell offers many more great options.

Here a blog post of mine which describes this in detail.

 

 

# Begin ----------------------------------------------------------------

."$PSScriptRoot\COM.ps1";

# Function Get-SessionList
Function Get-SessionList {

  Param(
    $sessionList
  )

  $SapGuiAuto = Get-Object "SAPGUI";
  If ($SapGuiAuto -IsNot [System.__ComObject]) {
    Return;
  }

  $Application = Invoke-Method $SapGuiAuto "GetScriptingEngine";
  If ($Application -IsNot [System.__ComObject]) {
    Return;
  }

  $Connections = Get-Property $Application "Connections";
  ForEach ($Connection In $Connections) {

    $Sessions = Get-Property $Connection "Sessions";
    ForEach ($Session In $Sessions) {

      If (-Not $Session.Busy()) {
        $Info = Get-Property $Session "Info";
        If ($Info -Is [System.__ComObject]) {
          $ActiveWindow = Get-Property $Session "ActiveWindow";
          $WindowName = Get-Property $ActiveWindow "Text";
          $SystemName = Get-Property $Info "SystemName";
          $sessionList += [PSCustomObject]@{
            WindowName = $WindowName
            SystemName = $SystemName
          }
        }
      }

    }

  }

  return $sessionList;

}

# Sub Main
Function Main {
  $sessionList = @();
  $sessionList = Get-SessionList($sessionList);
  ForEach ($session in $sessionList) {
    Write-Host $session;
  }
}

# Main
Main;

# End ------------------------------------------------------------------

 

 

In my test case three sessions are open, in session two the debugger is open and it is blocked. The PowerShell program works without any blocking problems. It detects the fourth session, the ABAP Debugger, and the session one and three. Here I use PowerShell Core as execution runtime, but it works also with PowerShell Windows, which is present in every Windows installation.

Good luck with your future automation projects with SAP GUI Scripting.

Best regards
Stefan

P.S. As you can see, the problem was already solved. But only after the years of writing, almost 12 years for this post and 6 years for the solution post, may I be forgiven for forgetting.

entry.title: Re: Calculating Working days between 2 dates SAP SAC

entry.title: Announcing SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 6th July 2024
entry.content:
• We are thrilled to invite you to SAP Inside Track (SIT) Bengaluru on July 6th, 2023! This iteration, we're bringing to you a unique opportunity, focusing exclusively on SAP Business AI—the frontier where innovation is happening, and the future is being shaped.  
  • We want to share with the SAP community everything about Business AI, from how to build AI solutions on SAP BTP, to discover how partners and customers are leveraging SAP Business AI solutions and explore various SAP solutions with embedded AI through various hands-on, insightful sessions and demos. 
  • SAP Inside Track 2024 – Business AI Edition aim is to offer CXOs, Leaders, Developers, Consultants, Architects, IT managers with a deep understanding of how to effectively utilize Business AI in conjunction with SAP products.  

This free event is your gateway to unlocking the full potential of SAP and AI in your organization. 

What is SAP Inside Track?   
SAP Inside Track is a community-driven event where SAP professionals, developers, consultants, architects, and leaders come together to share their knowledge and insights on various SAP-related topics. This grassroots initiative, organized by members of the SAP Community, provides a platform for fostering connections, staying updated on the latest SAP technologies, and gaining recognition for your technical expertise. 
Know more about SAP Inside Track Here!

Event Details:  

Date: July 6th, 2024 
Time: 8 AM - 5 PM IST 
Venue: SAP Labs India, 138, SAP Labs Rd, EPIP Zone, Whitefield, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560066 

Registration:  

Don't miss out on this incredible opportunity to be part of the largest SAP community event in Bengaluru. Register now for SAP Inside Track Bengaluru on July 6th, 2023. 

Register Now!  
Note: This is an in-person event only.

Agenda:  

This SAP Inside Track will be one-full-day exploration of SAP Business AI. You will: 

  • Master the art of building AI-powered applications on SAP Business Technology Platform.  
  • Discover innovative Business AI solutions from leading partners and  
  • witness the transformative power of Embedded AI through live demos across SAP solutions. 

Our event will feature numerous sessions led by experts from SAP, partners, and customers. In addition to these sessions, there will be a dedicated zone for hands-on workshops guided by our AI experts, as well as various demo booths showcasing AI use cases and solutions. 

  • The agenda is currently in the making, and a separate blog post will be published soon. Join the SAP Community Bengaluru group to stay updated! 

Speaker Registration:  

Registrations for speakers are now open! If your expertise lies in building AI applications on SAP BTP, creating innovative Business AI solutions, or sharing your experience with Embedded AI in SAP solutions like SAP S/4HANA and SuccessFactors, we welcome your contributions. We also welcome our Partners and Customers to join us in sharing their expertise and experience in Business AI and showcase the stellar work to the entire community.  

Register now if you want to be a speaker!  

  • More details are provided in this blog post, so please check that for further information on registration. 

Exclusive Sponsorship: 

With a record footfall of 4000+ community members across locations in last 12 months we are humbled to serve the ever-growing appetite of the community to Learn, Network and Grow. If you wish to join us in contributing to the community and want to have access as a sponsor, please drop in a comment below and we will reach out to you! This time we have exclusively bundled offerings to ensure that the sponsors get to not only showcase their content but also showcase their expertise to the entire community across locations including Bangalore, Gurgaon, Hyderabad and Pune. 

Join the Bengaluru SAP Community Group:  

If you're based in Bengaluru or anywhere in Karnataka, we invite you to join our vibrant Bengaluru SAP Community Group. Engage in meaningful discussions, share ideas, and collaborate with fellow SAP enthusiasts. Use the below link to join. 
https://groups.community.sap.com/t5/bengaluru/gh-p/bangalore Also follow our Twitter & LinkedIn for more updates about this & future community events or to share your ideas. 


Best 
Ranjeet

entry.title: Re: My Inbox error on Fiori launchpad
entry.content:
Hello,

You need to apply note https://me.sap.com/notes/2348671/E

Regards

entry.title: Re: Create Dynamic Entity Model in SAP Gateway
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you very much for sharing this information.

Drawback of this approach is it's not possible to make parallel requests at the same time because odata structure is changed on the fly.

You can try it making two calls at the same time.Its not easy to simulate it but here is what I did: I added endless loop to stop at get_entityset.This endless loop can be broken by checking a customization variable from a table to break. I made first request which stopped at endless loop than changed odata structure than made second request and let both requests to continue at the same time by breaking endless loop. Gateway returns error message for second call in this case, such as mapping error.

entry.title: Hi experts,how i can get forecasted customer aging from sap ?
API Hub Creation failed

How do I fix it? Is it for paid plans only?

Here's the project structure

Much Appreciated,

Reinhardt

entry.title: Re: HTTP 403 Forbidden error when posting a https url from postman to CPI
entry.content:
Thank you Priyanka.
As a beginner, this 403 error has troubled me for a fow hours.
But it was you who ultimately provided me with a simple and correct solution.

entry.title: Re: How to select a specific session to do SAP GUI Scripting activities inside it
entry.content:
Hello @ndionisio_giv,

I tried it also with Visual Basic for Application (VBA) but here it is the same. If the program executes in the for each loop the session which is blocked by the debugger, in line 31, the program stops until the session is no longer blocked. It seems that the behavior of PowerShell and Visual Basic in this case differs.

' Begin-----------------------------------------------------------------

Option Explicit

Sub Main()

  On Error GoTo errorHandler

  Dim SapGuiAuto, App, Connections, Connection, Sessions, Session, Info

  Set SapGuiAuto = GetObject("SAPGUI")
  If Not IsObject(SapGuiAuto) Then
    Exit Sub
  End If

  Set App = SapGuiAuto.GetScriptingEngine
  If Not IsObject(App) Then
    Exit Sub
  End If

  App.HistoryEnabled = False

  Set Connections = App.Connections()
  For Each Connection In Connections

    If Connection.DisabledByServer = True Then
      GoTo nextConnection
    End If

    Set Sessions = Connection.Sessions()
    For Each Session In Sessions
    
      If Not IsObject(Session) Then
        GoTo nextSession
      End If
    
      Set Info = Session.Info()
    
      If Session.Busy = False And Info.IsLowSpeedConnection = False Then

          Debug.Print Info.SystemName + " > " + Session.ActiveWindow.Text

      End If
    
nextSession:
    Next

nextConnection:
  Next

  App.HistoryEnabled = True

  Exit Sub

errorHandler:
  Debug.Print Err.Description

End Sub

' End-------------------------------------------------------------------

Maybe I'm missing something. But at the moment I don't see any possibility to handle blocking sessions with Visual Basic (for Applications). I assume it is similar with the Windows Scripting Host.

Best regards
Stefan

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule
entry.content:
Hello

Yes you can learn from BTP trial version and then add the Booster SAP BUILD and create a new project.

There are few courses on sap learning hub.

 

Regards

Raj

entry.title: Customer/Vendor Balance confirmation
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to get balance confirmations for customer and vendor transactions through SAP B1. Please guide me with the best solution.

entry.title: Re: How to select a specific session to do SAP GUI Scripting activities inside it
entry.content:
Hello @ndionisio_giv,

I tried it also with AutoIt, and here it works also.

; Begin ----------------------------------------------------------------

AutoItSetOption("MustDeclareVars", 1)

Func Main()

  Local $SapGuiAuto, $application, $connections, $connection
  Local $sessions, $session, $output

  $SapGuiAuto = ObjGet("SAPGUI")
  If Not IsObj($SapGuiAuto) Or @Error Then
    Return
  EndIf

  $application = $SapGuiAuto.GetScriptingEngine()
  If Not IsObj($application) Then
    Return
  EndIf

  $connections = $application.Connections()
  If Not IsObj($connections) Then
    Return
  EndIf

  For $connection In $connections

    If $connection.DisabledByServer = True Then
      ContinueLoop
    EndIf

    $sessions = $connection.Sessions()
    If Not IsObj($sessions) Then
      ContinueLoop
    EndIf

    For $session In $sessions

      If $session.Busy = True Then
        ContinueLoop
      EndIf

      If $session.Info.IsLowSpeedConnection = True Then
        ContinueLoop
      EndIf

      $output = $output & $session.Info.SystemName & " > " & _
        $session.ActiveWindow.Text & @CRLF

    Next
  
  Next

  MsgBox(0, "", $output)

EndFunc

; Main
Main()

; End ------------------------------------------------------------------

It seems that it is a VBScript respectively Visual Basic for Application specific problem.

Best regards
Stefan

entry.title: Simplified Emergency Maintenance Process in S4 HANA Asset Management...It`s genuinely simplified
entry.content:
This blog outlines the key aspects of Simplified Emergency Maintenance Process in S4 HANA Asset Management (Cloud & On-Premise). When a technical object has a malfunction, we must plan the required repair work, as well as document and confirm the maintenance work with root cause when it is done. SAP has introduced "Report and Repair Malfunction" functionality to support maintenance technician to process malfunction activities for effective management of malfunctions.

Fact Sheet: The Breakdown Maintenance can be performed using various standard processes, Viz:

  1. Corrective Maintenance (Standard Process) - Merge steps Create/Plan/Release into a single step.
  2. Reactive Maintenance (Using Phase Model) with scope ID 4HH in SAP Best Practices, where system skips Screening/Planning/Approval - App ID F1511A
  3. Emergency Maintenance, where all the steps are covered in one Fiori App ID F2023 - Scope ID BH2 in SAP Best Practices is using this app.

Moving ahead, I will walk you through the "Report and Repair Malfunction" using Fiori App ID F2023.

Three tiles are provided with App ID F2023, i.e.

  • Report Malfunction Tile - Is used to create malfunction reports
  • Manage Malfunction Reports Tile - Is used to have the list of malfunction reports that have already been created
  • Repair Malfunctions-My Job List Tile - Is used to have the list of work items which were assigned to Me and My Team.

My Business Use Case:

One of the cooling water circulation pumps in KILN area has got malfunction, where the Tyre Coupling of the pump damaged. For instance, I wear "Lead Maintenance Technician" hat and perform all the necessary steps that are required to capture the malfunction...let`s dive in... 

Phases of the Malfunction Report Work Item

 

One must have completed below tasks to leverage "Report Malfunction" functionality using Fiori App ID F2023

  • Synchronize business partners with employee code so that the lead technician can assign work items to different maintenance technicians and choose respective employee numbers from value help.

Employee Master:

Business Partner and Employee master synchronization:

 

Then assign the employee code to respective work center, so that each employee spent hours cost can be tracked in the workorder.

  • Overall status profile "MAINTJOB" for malfunction report

Events of Status Profile: These events are meant for updating malfunction work item status for each user action

 

for example: 

  • Assignment of overall status profile "MAINTJOB" to Notification Type:

  • Assignment of overall status profile "MAINTJOB" to Order Type:

  • Assignment of Notification Type to Malfunction Report origin against respective Planning Plant:

  • Assignment of Notification Type to Order Type:

Now, let us get into transaction details of "Report and Repair Malfunction",

Report Malfunction: Create a malfunction report with all the relevant information required.

System generates the maintenance order for the malfunction report and assigns the order type as per system configuration.                                           

Plan Malfunction Repair Work:

I have planned the repair work with all the resources that are needed,

  • Work Items with responsible work center
  • Spare parts needed for repair work - Based on the Technical Object BOM system displays the Product Ids, if no BOM exists, we can search the required product IDs and assign to the malfunction report

For Instance, as a lead maintenance technician, I am going to have below operations and assign to respective main work center (Maintenance Technician) and relevant effort details.

  1. Isolate Power Supply
  2. Process Clearance -Isolate all Inlet and Outlet valves
  3. Issue Safety Certificate
  4. Inspect and replace Tyre Coupling
  5. Perform alignment
  6. Enable Power Supply
  7. Enable Inlet and Outlet valves
  8. Trial run and capture vibration readings using SPM meter

I have chosen below Products for pump repair work,

Now I release the malfunction report, then the status of the work item gets updated as "Ready for Work",

Now, let us view the work order of the malfunction report (Work Order Operations & Planned Cost details) from the malfunction report screen,

Click on "Display Maintenance Order"

As my business case demands the tracking of spent hours cost of each employee in the work order of the malfunction report, I have set up "Activity Type" and assigned to respective Main Work Center to capture maintenance technician spent hours. System updated the planned cost for Internal Activity, and for the planned products.

Once the work has been assigned to respective maintenance technicians, each technician can either run the "Manage Malfunction Report"  or "Repair Malfunctions-My Job List" to update their respective work item status.

With this "Repair Malfunctions - My Jobs List", I have got multiple options to select the work items based on Jobs. I have selected "Mine Only" to get all the work items that  have been assigned to me.

I can process required work item by choosing respective work item.

For instance, I have chosen "Manage Malfunction Report", where I can narrow down the work items based on Work Centers which were assigned to each work item.

We can have personalized variants with respect to malfunction report attributes, so that it would become handy to process the work items -  I have narrowed down the list using Work Center filter criteria.

Confirmation of Work Item details:

System has got an option to pause the work if there is a dependency. For instance, I have to get the committed spare parts from engineering stores and that may take a while. So, I have decided to pause the work item.

when I paused the work item, system prompted to update the appropriate reason for the pause and to confirm the operation partially.

Now, I have received the spare parts from engineering stores. Now I have updated the work item status as "Put In Process" and carried out the work.

As my work is done at shop-floor, I have updated the work item status as "work Is Done".

Then, our team has recorded the time that we spent to complete each work item using "Record Time" option.

Confirm Malfunction Duration:

My team has captured the spent hours and now I have got to confirm Malfunction Duration, i.e. Malfunction Start Date, Time & Malfunction End Date, Time and specify if the technical object was broken down.

Document the Malfunction Details: 

As it is a breakdown, I have got to Specify the affected parts by choosing appropriate damage codes, cause codes and activities that were carried out at shop floor.

I have captured the root cause details and I have got to complete the "Malfunction Report" by clicking on option "Complete", then the malfunction report status becomes "Close Report".

then finally I have closed the malfunction report by clicking on option "Close Report".

Now, let us look at the maintenance order status and cost details: Order has been technically completed, and operations were confirmed - The actual cost has been updated with respect to material consumption and internal activity (Personal Hours).

Post completion of the malfunction report, it is obvious that maintenance team analyzes MTTR and MTBF of respective technical object. To get an overview of malfunction details, one should use Fiori App ID F2812.

That`s all about genuinely simplified emergency maintenance process in S4 HANA Asset Management.

🙂I Hope my blog provides useful insights to maintenance team and I look forward to seeing maintenance team using this simplified emergency maintenance very effectively to manage malfunctions.

 

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy.C

entry.title: Re: Extracting Data from S4HANA ODP to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello @deshmukh ,
Thanks for the comment.
Regarding the semantic usage let me tell you about the semantic usage based on my understanding.

Semantic Usage:
While you import any remote tables into datasphere, the default semantic usage is Relational dataset - it cannot be consumed directly to SAC for analytical purposes.

In order to consume this data from SAP datasphere to SAC, previously (Semantic usage - Analytical dataset) is the option but now its deprecated. As an alternative we can consume the imported remote table as a Fact and then create an analytical model with it.

By this way we can consume the data from SAP datasphere to SAC for analytical purposes.
I have gone through these semantic usages. Hope it's clear.
Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Top Features in May 2024
entry.content:
Hi Tuncay\Experts,

Thanks it was helpfull, Iam working on DATA MIGARTION project, need data requirements documentation to capture all existing Source system details (SAP Datasphere Dataflow and transformation).

Dinya

entry.title: Incorrect DG Status in Sales Order
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am experiencing an issue with the Dangerous Goods status message in our SAP S/4HANA system. When creating a sales order, the system displays the status as “OK - No Dangerous Goods; Not Relevant” for a specific finished goods DG material that is classified as Dangerous Goods. The correct status message should be “OK - Transport Allowed.”

In our product compliance and DG classification settings, all modes of transport are allowed for all compliance requirements. However, the system is not reflecting this correctly.

Any insights or solutions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you,
Ajit Dude

entry.title: SAP Signavio - License - Access
entry.content:
Hi All,

To implement SAP signavio in customer landscape, I have following questions:

1.What is the approach to get the license and what type of license(s) should be we have to use SAP Signavio

2. How to access the SAP signavio (not the trail version). Is the URL provided by SAP after purchasing the license or do we have to create the application in SAP BTP under Global account->subaccount->instance & subscription. I'm totally confused about how do we get the access for SAP Signavio? 

3. Is the URL's different for the different solutions (SAP signavio proccess explore, process insights, process intellegince,process manaer, journey modeler,process collaboration hub, process governance) in SAP Signavio. 

4. To get the access, is this similar to SAP CALM ( where SAP sends the url's in the mail for SAP CALM after registration and then we access the CALM and the identify provider).

5. After registration and getting the access to SAP signavio, where do we have to start? Do we have to register the S/4HANA systems or ECC systems - ofcourse I think so and start with rest of the solutions. But which solution we have to start with. Please correct me if I' wrong). Is it from SAP Signavio proccess explore, process insights, process intelligence, process manager, journey modeler,process collaboration hub, process governance

I referred few blogs and help.sap.com, but I couldn't find the basics about how to connect , provision access, license.

Please help with the above information.

 

Regards,

Satish

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Business Partner Replication
entry.content: we have a requirement to replicate BP information from non-sap system to S/4HANA, will the above steps describe work? or should we be doing something different in S/4HANA private edition. Please advise

entry.title: Supporting Partners in Guiding their Customers through Sustainability Requirements
entry.content:
The mandate of sustainability has become a fundamental requirement for companies worldwide. This shift is due to a growing awareness of the interconnectedness of environmental, social, and economic factors and the realization that sustainable practices are not only ethically imperative but also essential for long-term profitability and viability.

This is accompanied by a steady increase in the number and complexity of sustainability regulations. The regulations cover the disclosure of non-financial performance strategies, methods, and metrics to stakeholders, including investors, employees, customers, and the public. The practice includes environmental, social, and governance (ESG) metrics. Companies must come to terms with this; in doing so, they are confronted with many challenges. There is a huge number of ESG reporting frameworks and standards globally and the requirements are still evolving. The required data basis for ESG reporting often is lacking and the effort for recurring data collection is very high.

Implementing all aspects of sustainability requires a holistic approach within the company and anchoring it in all business processes and products. SAP supports companies on their path to transformation with a range of new sustainability-related functions offered with the SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises. The SAP Sustainability solution portfolio is oriented towards various industries but also includes central cross-industry solutions.

The SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) is the key solution for sustainability reporting, helping customers to holistically monitor, manage, and optimize their sustainability goals by collecting and integrating data from various corporate sources, including environmental, social, and governance (ESG) data. It´s the single point of truth for ESG across reporting standards, the technical foundation for data collection and processing, and open for integration with a variety of sources.

A further major and cross-industry solution is SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, which calculates a company's total carbon footprint and attributes emissions to products and corporate overhead, aiming to achieve a balanced emission level through all production stages.

Due to the prevailing market environment, SAP is planning to expand its certification program for outsourcing partners to include certification for SAP Sustainability solutions operations. We have evaluated this with a couple of partners in individual conversations and encountered high interest in the topic – some of their customers have already reached out to them asking for guidance regarding ESG reporting. Therefore, they feel a great need to build up expertise to support customers with projects and services accordingly. There was a consensus that the legal requirements are becoming stricter regarding the quality and quantity of data to be published.

The partners are preparing accordingly for the implementation and maintenance of SAP Sustainability solutions and the creation of add-ons, extensions, and integrations - to support customers in compiling the necessary data for reporting.

We derived key elements for the scope of the certification from the SAP solution portfolio for sustainability. The SAP experts for sustainability solutions gave essential support and input. As the certification also covers general competencies related to sustainability, it certainly confirms a comprehensive view and broad expertise about sustainability. After piloting this new service with selected partners, we will launch the SAP Sustainability solutions operations certification in 2025.

 

Today, there is no way around sustainable action. If companies want to remain competitive in the future, they must address the impact of their own business activities on the environment and society. Sustainability should not be seen as a mere reporting issue, but as an opportunity to secure the future of the company. Partners should be prepared to support their customers in this important topic. A corresponding sustainability certification gives the partner a better market positioning and an additional seal of quality for its offering.

Information on the certification program for outsourcing partners and an overview of all certified partners can be found on the SAP Outsourcing Operations Partner Guide.

entry.title: Re: SAP BW: Automatic sending of server files (al11) to a...
entry.content:
I think these are your options:

  • mount the target directory so it is available via AL11 (OS) and then you direct the OpenHub file to that folder
  • write an OS level script, which copies the file from the ABAP AS folder to the desired target folder and execute it once the file has been created. If you load via Process Chain, it has a variant with which you can start OS commands, so theoretically you can automate this

entry.title: Re: Multiple Delta - Delta with different selection on 0F...
entry.content:
Hi Vinay

 

The behavior is correct. The framework is designed in a way, that the delta is collected for the union of all init criterias. It's called a "shared delta queue".

For further details check the KBA:
3030649 - ODP DTP is fetching all data from source system and then filtering in BW

 

br

Roland

I want the dashboard to track and display the status of data imports (successful or failed), issue warnings, and provide other relevant information. Could you provide guidance or best practices on how to set up such a dashboard effectively?

entry.title: We are using the "sysScoreCardObjectivePortlet". Can we not have the block layout option in the bloc
entry.content:
The "sysScoreCardObjectivePortlet" portlet is used to show, add, delete the users objective in the people profile.

The requirement is to opt for the block layout options in these portlets with which we can toggle the view between list and table view. Is the block layout functionality not supported in some of the portlets?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Same Value from Request to Base Planning Level
entry.content:
Hi @victor_hugodebastosleo 

The system is by default working that way.

Request level is any level above the base level. So, Loading the planning view at any higher level is the request level and updating the values in the planning view is the data at request level. Hence, by default the data will be stored at the base level following the disaggregation configuration for that keyfigure.

Let me know if you need any further clarifications

Best Regards,

Riyaz

entry.title: Re: Fast track your SAP IBP Real time integration journey
entry.content: @MichaelMack Is it the same as Event Management tool in the SCM Box? Just a thought..

entry.title: CDS Watch fails with folder watch error after a while in SAP Build Code (BAS)
entry.content:
Hey team,

When using SAP Build Code in my trial, I am working on a Full Stack Application (CAPire App). Using the generated Run configuration, everything runs fine, but after a while (few seconds to few minutes) an error occurs which kills the run stack. I feel ther is a bug as the '.git' folder should never be watched.

Help is appriciated.

[cds] - my watch has ended.  
watch.js:121
Process exited with code 1

Uncaught Error Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, stat '/home/user/projects/supernewaiapp/.git/objects/maintenance.lock'
    at statSync (node:fs:1659:25)
    at <anonymous> (node:internal/fs/recursive_watch:160:28)
    at emit (node:events:518:28)
    at FSWatcher._handle.onchange (node:internal/fs/watchers:215:12)
    at callbackTrampoline (node:internal/async_hooks:130:17)
    --- FSEVENTWRAP ---
    at init (node:internal/inspector_async_hook:25:19)
    at emitInitNative (node:internal/async_hooks:202:43)
    at FSWatcher (node:internal/fs/watchers:193:18)
    at watch (node:fs:2463:15)
    at #watchFile (node:internal/fs/recursive_watch:156:21)
    at #watchFolder (node:internal/fs/recursive_watch:129:26)
    at <anonymous> (node:internal/fs/recursive_watch:179:26)
    at emit (node:events:518:28)
    at FSWatcher._handle.onchange (node:internal/fs/watchers:215:12)
    at callbackTrampoline (node:internal/async_hooks:130:17)

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite trial: Deploy of Integration Fl...
entry.content:
Hi,

Go to Monitor -> Manage integration content and undeploy the iflow.

Wait for sometime for it to become invisible in manage integration content. Once it is fully undeployed, deploy it again.

Moreover since yesterday, I am not able to deploy SBPA related any content(process, actions).. it always timed out. 

Tried creating a new trial, switching machines, clearing cache, different browsers but still the same since yesterday.

 

Please help @Archana 

 

Regards,

Aman

entry.title: SAP BPC Influence Portal
entry.content:
Dear SAP BPC customers,

With SAP BPC and its embedded, standard and SAP BW-IP/PAK models, we have a very powerful on-premise product for planning and reporting in the market. The SAP BPC Embedded- and Standard Models support consolidation functionality as well.

But cloud-based solutions have become more and more important in the meantime and so we decided some time ago to develop new cloud-based solutions for BI, planning, predictive and consolidation solutions. 

We have been establishing our strategic solution SAC as the solution for planning, BI and predictive for a few years and we have now decided to reduce the development of new functionalities in SAP BPC/4HANA to a minimum. For this reason, we are closing our influence portal for SAP BPC/4HANA on December 31, 2024. In the future, we will have limited investments to bug fixes, legal enhancements, etc. All innovations will be part of the SAC product in the future. Group reporting is our strategic solution for consolidation use cases.

In addition, SAP BPC/4HANA continues to follow our overall SAP maintenance strategy.

  • SAP Customer Influence
  • 2934895 - Maintenance for SAP BW/4HANA
  • SAP BW/4HANA to extend maintenance in alignment with SAP Business Suite and SAP S/4HANA
  • 3057202 - SAP Business Planning and Consolidation 2021 SP00, version for SAP BW/4HANA Central Note
  • SAP Business Planning and Consolidation (SAP BPC) Strategy
  • Update about the maintenance timelines for current releases of SAP Business Planning and Consolidation, version for SAP BW/4HANA 2021
  • Product Availability Matrix

Thanks in advance.

Stalin S Mario

entry.title: Re: BTP, ABAP Edition and use of flat files
entry.content:
You right that classic function modules as the ones you mentioned are not supported in SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

Instead you would have to use integration scenarios like the following:

Integrating SAP Document Management Service | SAP Help Portal

Kind regards,

Andre

entry.title: Re: Anyone at SAPPHIRE looking for consulting roles?
entry.content:
Hi Venkata... You didn't mention which courses or what type of IT interest you have. That's O.K.

Whatever interests you should identify the top customers' compelling issues... challenges... SAP (& 3rd party) technology solutions...outcomes...metrics... and requirements. And then with that said... many, many companies have SAP centers of excellence (COEs) in India (e.g., Bangalore) and beyond... Google some lists (e.g., Glassdoor) and check out this SAP consulting partner site and find SAP partners. Next... network for informational discussions... keep an eye on events/webcasts... Explore Sapphire 2024, SAP TechEd 2024 (sign up for announcements). Not sure if they will have a TechEd Bangalore conference, but definitely virtual.  Stay plugged into the SAP Community and this Career Corner group (I see many hints of job opportunities when I poke around the discussion threads)! Take a step back and see where your 25 years of IT experience can be best leveraged with value and impact....And of course... along the way you'll find job postings... hear of opportunities... so be ready with a great resume... and do your homework prior to any interviews!

Feel free to share more specific questions or comments...
Regards,
Rich
SAP Consultant / Amazon Author - Job Seeking Warriors - A Mentors Guide to Winning

entry.content:

entry.title: message in sxi_monitor in ECC status log version but in PI is sucessfully
entry.content:
Dear expert

I am facing a strange issue in my synchronous scenario: ECC(proxy)->PI->BPM(soap). 

 Some of the messages for this scenario are stuck in sxi_monitor with status "Log Version" (grey circle sign). Inside sxi_monitor traces, there is no response. but I can see everything has processed successfully in PI and already responded to the message to ECC.

I suspected it was because the parameter timeout in PI was too short. So I set a timeout of 5 minutes in channel.But this situation still happens. From the PI log, the message is responded  within 1 minute.

Please help how should i proceed. I have already spent half a day searching over SDN and other sites....

Thanks very much.

 

##SAP Process Orchestration #SAP S

I loved your insights into the concept of chaos engineering and its parallels to the approach adopted by SAP. Thanks for the crisp blog.

Regards,

Sharath

entry.title: Re: "Error Getting Function" during RFC connection in CPI
entry.content:
"HttpResponseException" as well as "Error Getting Function" don't look like RFC-related errors to me, more like an error on CPI level?! I assume, you are sending an RFC-XML document to CPI, CPI converts this into RFC-format and then sends the RFC call to the Cloud Connector using JCo?

In that case I recommend activating the JCo-trace in CPI in order to check, whether the call arrives on the JCo/RFC layer at all, or whether it errors out somewhere earlier in the HTTP receiver or XML converter of the CPI system.

I the case that JCo (via SCC) cannot find the function module in the backend (e.g. because it is not remote enabled), I would expect the error message "Function not found", but not "Error Getting Function"?!

entry.title: Re: How to delete Modified/Inactive version from abap program
entry.content:
In SAP ABAP Editor (SE80), you can easily discard your inactive changes without creating a transport. Here's how:

1- In SE80, navigate to your ABAP program screen.
2- Look for the "Version" menu (usually on the top menu bar).
3- Within the "Version" menu, select "Version Management".
4- This will display a list of versions for your program.
5- Identify the "Inactive" version you want to discard (usually the latest one).
6- Right-click on the inactive version and choose "Toggle active and inactive version".

This action will discard your un-activated changes and revert the program back to its original state. You won't create any unnecessary transport requests and your program will be back to how it was before your modifications.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to define an input as an optional fiel...
entry.content:
I'm not exactly sure what you are asking, but yes, a Decision activity needs to have an input. Generally, you just define something, bind something to that input, and then write a condition true = true

I imagine soon there will be something like an expression editor where you will no longer need Decisions for this purpose.

entry.title: Re: Need to ALE only 2 fields through a reduced message type copied from MATMAS
entry.content:
While 17 years since the original question, if anyone is interested, the list of this mandatory fields is stored in table TBD23 and you can edit the contents via SM30 using associated view V_TBD23.

BD53 reads this information in the underlying report RBDIDOCR using form GET_DATA into a local table FIELD_LIST (search for "FIELD_LIST-TYPE ='M'. " -> 'M' is the constant for compulsory field).

entry.title: Application type for UI5 apps in SU22
entry.content:
Hi experts,

What's the application type for UI5 apps in SU22?

It looks like OData v4 is the one for Fiori element based UI5 apps, but what's the application type for free style UI5 apps? Maybe BSP I guess?

Regards,

Eric

somehow it is not possible to finish this tutorial:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/api-hub-creation-failed/qaq-p/13718945

entry.title: Re: Connecting Splash BI tool to S4HANA and BW4HANA
entry.content:
I suppose this is only possible via direct access to the HANA-DB (ODBC)
or with 3rd-party-tools like Theobald Xtract

entry.title: Payment Term configuration not obtaining the desired result
entry.content:
I need to create a new Payment Terms that involves that payment will be made after 30 days on the 10th of the month.

For example if the Document date is 18.05.2024 calculated due date should be 10.07.2024

Document date 18.05.2024 + 30 days --> 17.06.2024--> 10.07.2024 (day 10th)

Another example if the Document date is 02.06.2024 calculated due date should be 10.07.2024 as well

Document date 02.06.2024 + 30 days --> 02.07.2024 --> 10.07.2024 (day 10th)

I have done many different configurations for the new Payment Terms but I don’t get any combination to calculate net due date 10.07.2024 in both cases when Document date is 18.05.2024 and 02.06.2024.

Do you know if there is a way to configure the Payment Terms to get the result that I need?
